title: Invariants of Lattice Polytopes and Matroids
speaker: Eric Katz
abstract: We will discuss invariants of lattice polytopes and their subdivisions arising from Ehrhart and Hodge theory and introduce their matroid theoretic analogues which are enriched versions of the characteristic and Tutte polynomials.
tags: Workshop on Combinatorics of Enumerative Geometry

proof_stages.label: Ehrhart-Hodge template for lattice polytopes
proof_stages.summary: The talk frames the problem of finding a matroid analogue of Ehrhart theory, then recalls that for a lattice polytope the Ehrhart series, h-star polynomial, and generic Newton-polytope hypersurfaces are linked through the Hodge filtration on primitive middle cohomology.

proof_stages.label: Toric K-theory computation of h-star
proof_stages.summary: The speaker sketches the standard toric proof: on a smooth toric compactification with boundary, log cotangent and ideal-sheaf exact sequences reduce the chi-y characteristic of the open hypersurface to Euler-characteristic computations of toric line bundles, recovering the h-star polynomial from lattice-point counts.

proof_stages.label: Local h-star, reciprocity, and subdivision refinements
proof_stages.summary: The open h-star polynomial is paired with a symmetric local h-star polynomial coming from intersection cohomology of the compactified hypersurface, related by inclusion-exclusion over faces, and degenerating families motivate two-variable h-star invariants of regular lattice subdivisions.

proof_stages.label: Matroid dictionary via wonderful compactifications
proof_stages.summary: The toric dictionary is transferred to realizable matroids by replacing the algebraic torus with a hyperplane-arrangement complement, toric compactifications with wonderful compactifications, and the toric ample class with the pullback of the permutahedral line bundle; the characteristic polynomial is identified as the open chi-y invariant with a symmetric compactification counterpart.

proof_stages.label: Permutahedral polynomial and valuativity
proof_stages.summary: As an Ehrhart-count analogue, the permutahedral polynomial counts sections of powers of the permutahedral class on the wonderful compactification, is combinatorial and valuative by K-theoretic results on matroids, and can be identified with independent-set counts in a modified Dilworth truncation, implying ultra-log-concavity.

proof_stages.label: K-theoretic permutahedral enrichment
proof_stages.summary: The speaker explains how to enrich matroid invariants by computing Euler characteristics on the wonderful compactification via classes on the permutahedral toric variety, using tautological K-classes for the fundamental class and log cotangent bundle, then inserting variables and powers of the permutahedral line bundle to define a three-variable permutahedral matroid invariant.

proof_stages.label: Structural properties of the permutahedral matroid invariant
proof_stages.summary: The new invariant is stated to be valuative, to satisfy a form of Ehrhart reciprocity or acceptability, to specialize to permutahedral Tutte and characteristic invariants, and to obey matroid duality, although a clean combinatorial characterization remains open.

proof_stages.label: Permutahedral h-star polynomial and open problems
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines a matroidal analogue of the h-star polynomial from the primitive middle cohomology of a generic hypersurface in the permutahedral class, gives its K-theoretic formula and expected positivity/reciprocity properties, and ends with open problems about symmetric cousins, algebraic models, deletion-contraction, localization, activity formulas, and subdivision invariants.

examples.name: snake matroids as valuative generators
examples.note: Snake matroids are mentioned as a concrete test family on which one could compute the invariant because they generate the valuative group of matroids, illustrating how valuativity might lead to a characterization.
examples.engagement_reason: Snake matroids are cited as valuative generators and as a future route to characterize the invariant, but no invariant is computed on them.

passes.reason: snake matroids are used as a systematic valuative generating family, not as sporadic, limiting, extremal, symmetric, or refuting examples
Snake matroids are invoked as a systematic family of valuative generators, not as a failure case or a structurally degenerate/extreme/exceptional object.

examples.name: Boolean matroid special case
examples.note: In response to a question, the speaker says that for the Boolean matroid the construction reduces to a toric computation, namely the h-star polynomial of the permutahedron, serving as a sanity-check special case.
examples.engagement_reason: The Boolean matroid is raised as a special case and identified informally with computing the h-star polynomial of the permutahedron, but the speaker does not carry out the computation.

passes.reason: the Boolean/free matroid is a highly symmetric boundary case with no dependence relations and maximal rank, used only as a computable special case
The Boolean matroid is the free matroid with no dependence relations, an extreme maximal-rank base case rather than a counterexample.
The Boolean matroid U_{n,n} is the free boundary case with a single basis/base polytope point, used only as a special computation.
The Boolean/free matroid is the maximally free uniform matroid with no dependence structure, used only as a basic special case check.
The Boolean/free matroid is the maximally symmetric no-dependence endpoint, used only as a computable special case.
The Boolean/free matroid is the maximal-rank all-independent case with collapsed dependence structure, used only as a computable special case.
The Boolean/free matroid is used as a computable special case; structurally it is the maximally free rank-full matroid with no dependencies and full ground-set symmetry.
The Boolean/free matroid is a maximally independent boundary case with a collapsed one-basis structure and full ground-set symmetry.
The Boolean/free matroid is used as a computable special case; structurally it is the maximally independent, collapsed unique-basis edge case.
The Boolean/free matroid is the maximally symmetric no-dependence uniform extreme, used only as an easy special case to compute.
The Boolean/free matroid is the maximally independent uniform boundary case with full element symmetry, used only as a simple specialization.
The Boolean/free matroid U_{n,n} is the all-independent endpoint with one basis and full ground-set symmetry, used only as a computable special case.

examples.name: generalized permutohedron line bundles
examples.note: An audience question asks about replacing the standard permutahedral class by classes from generalized permutohedra, illustrating a possible nonessential variation of the construction and a future computational direction.
examples.engagement_reason: Generalized permutohedron line bundles are discussed as possible alternative choices of class, but no construction or calculation is run for a specific such bundle.

passes.reason: generalized permutohedron line bundles form an ordinary systematic family of possible variants, raised as an extension rather than an obstruction

Generalized-permutohedron line bundles are a broad systematic alternative to the standard permutahedral class, not a refuting example.
